title: Unlocking Talent Potential in the Age of AI
excerpt: By Meenakshi Dabral, CHRO, MovingWalls Group Why Human-Centred Cultures Will Lead the AI-Powered Future Work today isn’t defined by job titles, office hours, or where you sit. It’s defined by doing w...
content:
By Meenakshi Dabral, CHRO, MovingWalls Group

Why Human-Centred Cultures Will Lead the AI-Powered Future

Work today isn’t defined by job titles, office hours, or where you sit. It’s defined by doing work that matters and work with purpose. The evolution of the workplace isn’t up for debate anymore; it’s a lived reality. What matters now is how we shape what comes next with clarity, care, and the courage to lead forward.

For organizations at the forefront of change, this moment is more than a shift and it’s an invitation to reimagine how we work, grow, and define success. The future belongs to companies that prioritize people. Because here’s the truth: technology doesn’t transform companies people do. But when organizations embrace AI strategically, it becomes one of the most powerful enablers of that human transformation.

AI is no longer just a tool, it’s a talent multiplier that allows organizations to listen better, personalize development, and respond to evolving needs with precision and empathy. When woven into HR systems and decision-making, AI can identify emerging leaders, recommend tailored learning paths, reduce bias, and support career mobility in real time. Done right, AI becomes a quiet force behind the scenes in uncovering potential, guiding growth, and enhancing performance. But it does not replace human judgment. Instead, it supports leaders in making more equitable, data-informed, and compassionate decisions. That’s the real power of AI: to augment human leadership, not automate it.

Employees today are seeking not just for a job, they’re seeking growth. They want roles that challenge them, work that aligns with their values, and career journeys that build their future. According to LinkedIn’s 2024 Workplace Learning Report, 89% of L&D leaders say building employee skills is essential to navigating the future of work. McKinsey adds that employees who feel their skills are underused are ten times more likely to leave.

The World Economic Forum projects that 44% of core job skills will change by 2027, largely due to AI, automation, and the ongoing evolution of business models. Yet only half of workers globally are expected to be prepared. This is not just a workforce development issue, its a wake-up call for leadership. A future-ready workforce is not built by chance; it’s shaped by design. And that starts with embracing a skills-first culture, a one where career paths are fluid, learning is continuous, and advancement doesn’t require permission.

Organizations that treat growth as a shared responsibility unlock more than performance, they unlock potentials. AI can help spotlight capability gaps, match people with stretch opportunities, and surface hidden talent before it walks out the door. It provides insight into what employees need, even before they ask for it. But unlocking talent is about more than deploying platforms and it’s about designing experiences where people feel seen and supported. It’s about creating spaces for bold ideas and building trust through consistent, human-centred leadership.

“The real future of work isn’t defined by roles because it’s powered by growth, guided by trust, and fuelled by courage.”

This approach drives both engagement and retention. According to Gallup, only 23% of employees worldwide are engaged at work, and just 33% say they’re thriving in their overall well-being. These numbers are reflections of cultural disconnects. People leave when they feel stagnant. They stay when they feel they are evolving.

While performance metrics remain important, they are no longer enough. Real progress is now measured in courage, creativity, and contributions.. The best cultures recognize that presence matters as much as output. They reward curiosity, value emotional intelligence, and celebrate growth, not just achievements. Leadership today is about lifting others and fostering environments where people are free to challenge norms, test new ideas, and move across roles without waiting for permission.

In this new world of work, retention has a different meaning. It’s not only about locking people in but also its about giving them a reason to stay. That’s why our investment in people goes far beyond onboarding. We build meaningful, personalized growth journeys. Whether it’s through learning opportunities, recognition, mentorship, or simply creating room to be heard, our focus is on helping individuals build careers as dynamic as the world around them.

Culture is no longer something we write on walls. It’s lived through transparency, trust, and speed. It shows up in how we support each other, how we collaborate, and how we lead. Because at the heart of it, people don’t leave companies, they leave cultures, and they stay when they feel empowered, challenged, and valued. The future of work is not just about policies, offices, or technologies. It’s about people. It always has been. But now we have better tools to support them, amplify their growth, and guide their journey. The companies that thrive in the AI era will not be those with the most automation but those with the most ambition to empower their people. We’re not waiting for the future to arrive. We’re shaping it with empathy, intelligence, and the belief that when people are empowered to lead, everything else follows.

About the Author

Meenakshi Dabral is the Chief Human Resources Officer at MovingWalls. Group, Meenakshi Dabral is Chief Human Resources Officer at MovingWalls. A champion of bold, human-centred transformation, she guides organizations through the dynamic intersection of data-driven strategy, inclusive leadership, and cultural innovation. With over two decades of global experience across industry giants like Citigroup, GE, and Mercedes, as well as high-growth fintech start-ups and entrepreneurial ventures, Meenakshi has led transformative HR initiatives that fuel business growth and cultural excellence. She brings extensive expertise in people, culture, and organizational development—helping businesses unlock performance, scale with purpose, and build thriving, future-ready teams.
author: dinesh

title: Unlocking Retail Growth: Insights from the Last Mile Advertising Panel at NRF APAC 2025
excerpt: Retail is undergoing a profound transformation and at the center of this shift  is “last mile advertising” . This refers to how retailers and brands engage consumers at the point of purchase, usi...
content:
Retail is undergoing a profound transformation and at the center of this shift  is “last mile advertising”. This refers to how retailers and brands engage consumers at the point of purchase, using data-driven in-store media to influence decision-making and drive measurable revenue.

This was the central theme at the “Last Mile Advertising – Using Data and In-Store Media to Drive Retail Growth” panel, hosted by MovingWalls during the NRF APAC 2025 in Singapore, featuring experts from across the ecosystem:

  • Junichi Eto, Managing Director, JRE Ventures
  • Richard Murray, CEO, ADM Group
  • May Suenikom, Global Marketing Director, Coffee Bean & Tea Leaf

The session unpacked how retail media is rapidly evolving from a tactical execution layer into a strategic growth engine, viewed through the lenses of investment, marketing services, and brand leadership. 

Here’s what we learned.

Key Takeaways

  • Retail media is shifting from a cost center to a profit-driving asset, unlocking new revenue streams and elevating in-store engagement.
  • Audience and transaction data are critical for precision targeting and personalization, making retail media more intelligent than traditional digital channels.
  • Despite the rise of digital channels, in-store environments remain dominant, with 87% of purchase decisions still made offline.
  • Organizational change is necessary: from siloed merchandising/media teams to integrated retail media strategy units.
  • APAC shows varying maturity: Australia leads in adoption, while Southeast Asia is poised for rapid growth.

Investor Perspective: Turning Infrastructure into Innovative Media

Junichi Eto, Managing Director at JRE Ventures (Japan’s government-backed venture capital firm) highlighted the macroeconomic context in Japan, where organic retail growth is slowing. Retail media, he noted, is emerging as a growth lever and presents a path forward for value creation.

JRE, long known for its railway business, is now introducing digital media into train stations to take advantage of foot traffic and location data. Combining demographic information with transaction history enables more precise targeting than traditional digital media.

“Retail media will become the dominant media category—because it uses real purchase data, not just intent.”
— Junichi Eto, JRE Ventures

Eto emphasized that retail media will soon be the dominant media category because it integrates first-party data, in-store visits, and actual purchasing behavior. This enables:

  • Better product discovery
  • Stronger retailer-brand relationships
  • More intelligent marketing strategies

However, he also pointed out a key structural challenge: silos between merchandising and media teams. Bridging these divisions is crucial for retailers to maximize the value of their media assets and improve the customer journey. To unlock full-funnel value, organizations must integrate both functions into a single retail media framework.

Marketing Services Perspective: Scaling Personalization In-Store

Richard Murray, CEO of ADM Group, spoke about ADM’s investments across Asia in retail media networks, particularly through retail groups with massive offline reach.

ADM has built one of the most sophisticated retail media infrastructures in Asia, combining:

  • Street-level sensors to track dwell time, impressions, gender, and conversions
  • AI + loyalty program data to personalize offers across pre-store, in-store, and post-store touchpoints

ADM is helping brands customize campaigns across the customer journey, including before, during, and after the store visit.

“87% of purchases still happen offline. That’s where intent lives.”

— Richard Murray, ADM

Retail media, Murray noted, provides incremental revenue with high margins for retailers, and deep performance insights for brands. Critically, he reminded the audience that 87% of all purchase decisions are made in-store, making last-mile engagement an intuitive place to invest.

ADM’s approach demonstrates that in-store activation isn't just awareness; it also drives conversion, measurement, and long-term retention.

Brand Perspective: Owning the Moment of Truth

May Suenikom, Global Marketing Director at Coffee Bean & Tea Leaf, focused on experience continuity across both physical and digital channels for a seamless customer experience.

“Impulse matters. With products like ice cream, the last mile is everything.”

— May Suenikom, Global Marketing Director, Coffee Bean & Tea Leaf

Retail media, she noted, plays a critical role in the “moment of truth,” which is the point when a consumer is deciding whether to buy or move on to the next option. For impulse categories like beverages or desserts, timing is crucial. Suenikom relies on foot traffic monitoring and in-store analytics to ensure campaigns are timely, relevant, and personalized.

In her view, real-time engagement at the point of sale is make-or-break. Retail media is no longer optional; it’s essential for building brand equity and driving conversions.

Market Dynamics & Regional Maturity

The panel also addressed the APAC retail media maturity spectrum:

  • Australia is leading with sophisticated network deployment and analytics integration.
  • Southeast Asia is catching up fast, driven by digital-savvy consumers and rising brand demand.
  • Japan is taking a data-infrastructure-first approach, integrating retail into transport and public utilities.

Despite different starting points, the shared vision is clear: retail media will become central to omnichannel strategy in APAC.

Retail Media: From Execution Layer to Growth Engine

Retail media has moved beyond campaign execution to become a strategic growth driver by enabling brands to build equity through relevant in-store engagement, monetize data and physical space for new revenue streams, and deliver personalized commerce at scale, making it central to modern omnichannel strategy.

With access to tools like heat maps, foot traffic analytics, and in-store behavioral insights, brands can now apply the same performance marketing mindset to physical retail spaces that they use in platforms like Google Ads or Meta Ads Manager. Just as marketers A/B test creatives or optimize bidding strategies based on CTRs and conversion paths online, they can now:

  • Test creative placements in-store by tracking which screen locations drive higher dwell times and conversions.
  • Use real-time footfall data to adjust promotions by time of day, mirroring dayparting strategies used in search and display campaigns.
  • Deploy dynamic content on digital signage based on customer segments, similar to audience-based personalization in email or programmatic display.
  • Attribute sales lift to in-store impressions, just as brands measure ROAS online using pixel tracking.

For example, a consumer electronics brand running a campaign across multiple mall locations can analyze which screen placements drive the most traffic to their nearby pop-up or retail shelf. They can then reallocate media budget or change content frequency, just like optimizing budget allocation across keywords or audiences in Google Ads.

In this new environment, the store becomes a high-performing channel, not just a sales floor. It can be measured, optimized, and scaled like any other media investment.

Final Thought: Time to Rethink the Last Mile

As the panelists made clear, the future of retail growth lies in the last mile—where physical interaction meets data precision. Retail media delivers value across the board:

  • For retailers: new revenue and shopper insight
  • For brands: relevance and ROI
  • For consumers: more tailored, valuable experiences

Organizations that move quickly to break silos, unify data, and invest in programmable in-store media will shape the next wave of retail transformation across APAC and beyond.

Interested in launching your own retail media strategy?
Connect with MovingWalls to explore our last mile activation toolkit.
author: dinesh

content:
Is Your OOH Inventory Just Playing Ads or Proving Value?

Let’s face it, running digital or static OOH ads is only half the job. The real challenge begins after the campaign goes live. Advertisers today aren’t just paying for exposure; they’re paying for performance and expect media owners to deliver proof of impact. So here’s the big question:

Can you confidently prove that your inventory is driving real value?

Why Media Owners Are Still Left in the Dark

For media owners, the silence that follows a campaign launch can be deafening. Advertisers want fast, reliable, and measurable insights. But most traditional reporting tools aren’t built for that. Here's what you're probably facing:

  • Limited visibility into how inventory performs in real-time
  • No centralized dashboard to track audience behavior

This often results in a constant struggle to validate ROI especially for direct advertisers and agency clients who expect measurable proof of performance.

Sound familiar?

Despite the growing pressure for accountability, many media owners are still relying on outdated methods like footfall estimates or assumed impressions. That might have worked in the past, but advertisers today want more. They expect:

  • Verified numbers
  • Ongoing visibility
  • Clear, performance-linked reporting

According to the World Out of Home Organization’s 2024 Global Expenditure Survey, global OOH ad spend hit $41.9 billion in 2023 and is projected to reach $45.3 billion in 2024. The industry is growing fast but so is the pressure to prove campaign impact.

Meet the Tool That Turns Screens Into Smart Media

MovingWalls Measure specifically helps media owners automate, optimize, and validate inventory performance in real time.

Whether you're managing transit ads, digital billboards, or static screens, MovingWalls Measure turns passive inventory into measurable, accountable media assets. 

How MovingWalls Measure Drive Advertiser Confidence for your Media Inventory

Understand Audience Behavior

Prove Business Outcomes

Why Advertisers Come Back (Not Just Buy Once)

When you can show advertisers real results not just impressions they see you as more than a media supplier. They see you as a growth partner. And that’s how trust is built.

With MovingWalls Measure, you can:

  • Win repeat business with transparent reporting

Turn Attention Into Action. Turn Ads Into Outcomes.

  • Your screens already capture attention now let them capture results.

Start measuring what truly matters today.

title: DOOH Advertising: How OOH Media Teams Can Evolve with Programmatic and Audience-First Strategies
excerpt: Neon Nancy had been in the business for decades. She made her name selling premium billboard locations, convincing brands that 50,000 daily views was all they needed to know. Location was king, and sh...
content:
Neon Nancy had been in the business for decades. She made her name selling premium billboard locations, convincing brands that 50,000 daily views was all they needed to know. Location was king, and she was the queen of the neon-lit skyline.

Then came QR Code Quincy.

A digital marketing expert obsessed with bridging physical and digital experiences, Quincy saw the game changing fast. He wasn’t interested in just selling locations, he was pioneering digital out-of-home advertising (DOOH) that focused on audiences, real-time engagement, and data-driven insights.

Nancy’s Wake-Up Call

It all came to a head when Nancy lost a major deal. A global sneaker brand had always booked her biggest digital board downtown, until they suddenly pulled out.

Nancy stormed into the brand’s office. “Why the switch?” she demanded.

Their response? “We need real engagement data, audience targeting, and interactive capabilities. Quincy’s network delivers that. Your billboards? They’re just big screens.”

Nancy was stunned. She had digital billboards, but she was still selling them the old way, by location, not by audience.

Quincy’s Playbook: The New Rules of OOH

Quincy sat Nancy down and broke it down for her.

✔ Sell audience, not space: Brands don’t just want to know how many people might see an ad. They want demographics, behaviors, and engagement metrics.

✔ Use programmatic advertising: Real-time bidding lets brands buy ad slots dynamically, targeting the right people at the right moment.

✔ Make billboards interactive: Adding QR codes, mobile integrations, and personalized content turns static screens into engagement hubs.

✔ Adopt a full tech stack: Booking systems, CMS platforms, audience measurement tools, the right tech drives smarter sales.

✔ Measure real-world impact: Foot traffic, brand lift, store visits—advertisers want data that proves their investment is driving real results.

Nancy’s Transformation

It wasn’t easy, but Nancy evolved. Inspired by Quincy and sessions at the WOO Global Congress, she made major changes:

✅ Built a programmatic DOOH advertising sales team to interface with digital media buyers
✅ Integrated audience analytics into her pitches using smart data platforms
✅ Launched interactive billboard campaigns with QR codes and geotargeting
✅ Shifted her messaging from presence to performance-based OOH media sales

The result? Bigger deals, happier advertisers, and a billboard business built for the future.

The Takeaway: Adapting to the New OOH Landscape

The out-of-home advertising industry is changing fast. Every team includes sales, marketing, operations, and technology, must evolve to stay relevant.

From Location-Based Sales to Audience-Driven Strategies

Traditionally, OOH media owners sold based on prime locations and foot traffic estimates. But today’s advertisers expect:

  • Real-time audience insights
  • Programmatic ad buying
  • Measurable engagement and interactivity

How Teams Need to Evolve

1. Sales: From Static Selling to Smart Data

  • Focus on audience reach, not just impressions.
  • Adopt programmatic advertising and CRM automation.
  • Use self-serve platforms to attract digital budgets.

2. Operations: From Basic Logistics to Digital Optimization

  • Manage real-time content scheduling and ad-serving tools.
  • Optimize inventory availability and pricing with data.

3. Technology: The Backbone of Modern OOH

  • Move beyond basic CMS systems to advanced ad tech.
  • Build or integrate data platforms for campaign tracking.

4. Marketing: From Awareness to Engagement & Data-Driven Growth

  • Leverage CRM, martech, and inbound marketing to generate leads.
  • Use QR codes, AR experiences, and mobile retargeting to drive conversions.

The Bottom Line

Out-of-home advertising is no longer just about big screens in prime locations. The future belongs to media owners who can offer data-driven insights, sell audience reach, and create interactive experiences.

The question is: Are you ready?
author: dinesh

title: How Creativity Meets Data: Building High-Impact OOH Campaigns
excerpt: The Campaign That Changed Everything Neon Nancy stared at the glowing dashboard in disbelief. Her latest masterpiece, a hand-painted perfume ad she'd spent months perfecting, was underperforming. The...
content:
The Campaign That Changed Everything

Neon Nancy stared at the glowing dashboard in disbelief. Her latest masterpiece, a hand-painted perfume ad she'd spent months perfecting, was underperforming. The static billboard along Sunset Boulevard was getting decent views, but something was missing.

Across the office, AI Amy monitored the real-time analytics. "Nancy, your creative is stunning," she said gently, "but it's not connecting."

Nancy bristled. "What do you mean? It's art!"

Amy pulled up the numbers:

  • Dwell time: 7 seconds (industry average: 10)
  • Engagement: Minimal interactions
  • Conversions: Almost none

Then Amy showed her something extraordinary—a heat map of pedestrian traffic around the billboard. Crowds were pausing... but walking right past.

The Experiment That Redefined Their Strategy

Reluctantly, Nancy agreed to test the power of data-driven OOH advertising:

  • Week 1: Her original static ad ran unchanged
  • Week 2: Amy added a simple dynamic layer—a QR code for a free sample
  • Week 3: They introduced a dynamic OOH campaign with context-aware creative:
    • Morning: "Your signature scent for the day ahead"
    • Evening: "Find your nighttime fragrance"
    • Rainy days: "Scent that lasts, no matter the weather"

The Result: A Creative and Data-Driven Win

By the end of the third week:

✅ Dwell time increased by 300%

 ✅ QR code scans exceeded 1,000

 ✅ Nearby store traffic rose by 25%

Nancy stared at the dashboard again—this time with a smile. "Okay... maybe data isn't the enemy."

Why This Works: The Data Behind the Story

The most successful OOH campaigns today combine creative and data in OOH strategies. Here's how:

1. Precision Targeting Replaces Guesswork

Modern OOH leverages:

  • Mobile location data to measure true viewership
  • Computer vision to assess engagement patterns
  • Market-specific insights ( transit ads perform best in Singapore, roadside dominates in the US)

2. Dynamic Creative Outperforms Static

When ads respond to their environment, they perform better:

  • Weather, time, and location-triggered ads see 38% better performance
  • Real-world example: A burger chain changing menu items on digital boards based on traffic and weather data

3. Measurable Impact at Every Stage

Today’s OOH is measurable across the full funnel:

  • Brand lift and recall improvements
  • Audience activation via QR codes and downloads
  • Sales conversions linked to store visits after ad exposure

Retail brands are leading the way, blending in-store media with outdoor activations powered by OOH ad optimization techniques.

Your Playbook for Future-Proof OOH

As the WOO Congress approaches, here’s how to bring your OOH strategy into the next era:

As the WOO Congress, one of the largest events in the OOH industry, approaches with global leaders convening in Mexico, here’s how to bring your OOH strategy into the next era:

Start With Bold Creative

Nancy's art-first approach still matters, it's the foundation of impactful outdoor storytelling.

Add Smart Layers

Begin with one dynamic element, start with a QR code in your billboard ad or use dayparting to make your campaigns more responsive.

Measure Beyond Impressions

Track what really counts: dwell time, engagement, and conversions.

Optimize Relentlessly

Use performance insights to guide your next creative iteration through consistent OOH ad optimization.

“The future isn’t choosing between art and algorithms - it’s letting them work together.” — Neon Nancy

title: From Street Stunt to Strategic Breakthrough: How a Claw Machine Redefined OOH in Mexico City
excerpt: By Srikanth Ramachandran In May 2025, something unusual appeared overnight on the side of a building in downtown Mexico City: a giant claw machine. No branding, no permits, no explanation. By morning,...
content:
By Srikanth Ramachandran

In May 2025, something unusual appeared overnight on the side of a building in downtown Mexico City: a giant claw machine. No branding, no permits, no explanation.

By morning, crowds had gathered. Some lined up to play. Others pulled out their phones. Social media buzzed with curiosity and videos. Authorities prepared to intervene.

Then Nike called.

What began as a rogue stunt quickly evolved into a data-powered activation now regarded as a landmark moment in the transformation of out-of-home (OOH) advertising.

Behind the spectacle was Gael Guerrero, a well known creative provocateur famous for bold urban interventions. Watching from across the street was Santiago Márquez, a veteran media strategist with two decades of experience in traditional OOH planning. Gael brought the disruption. Santiago saw the data opportunity. Together, they forged an unlikely alliance, one that bridged artistic risk with commercial scale.

“What I saw wasn’t vandalism,” Santiago said. “It was untapped potential.”

Within 72 hours, the installation was legalized. Sponsors came on board. Nike became the lead partner. The claw machine was reengineered with RFID-tagged prizes, facial analytics to understand audience engagement, and mobile retargeting to extend the experience beyond the street.

“The claw machine wasn’t just a stunt. It was a signal,” said Gael. “A glimpse of what OOH can become when it’s brave enough to play.”

The results were immediate and measurable: 2.1 million social impressions, more than 18,000 participant sign-ups, a 37% increase in Nike sales at nearby stores, and a six-month citywide activation confirmed within the week.

What started as a spectacle became a blueprint and proof that OOH can be immersive, data-informed, and business-driving.

“Hasta los rebeldes necesitan seguro médico.” — Gael Guerrero

This moment was more than just a media stunt. It was a signal that OOH is rapidly evolving from static formats to dynamic, interactive experiences grounded in technology and real-time data.

“Today, the most effective OOH campaigns were seen and they were shared, measured and felt.”

OOH is now:

  • Guided by audience movement data for smarter placements
  • Activated with contextual and real triggers like traffic and weather
  • Enhanced by vision and mobile analytics
  • Measured by attribution tools that connect exposure to outcomes

“OOH is no longer background noise. It’s foreground engagement.”

People no longer want to simply see ads because they want to feel something. They want experiences worth sharing. The claw machine worked because it created a participatory moment that blurred the lines between entertainment, media, and retail.

For the global OOH community, the lesson is clear: the future belongs to formats that combine creative daring with technical precision. The claw machine may have started as an act of rebellion, but it became a case study in what’s possible when innovation, data, and cultural curiosity collide.

What happened on that street corner in Mexico City wasn't just a campaign. It was a glimpse into the next era of OOH.
author: dinesh

title: From Steakhouse Deals to Self-Service: How OOH's Buyer Landscape Changed
excerpt: Let me tell you about the lunch that changed everything for Billboard Bob. Last Tuesday, Bob took his favorite client – the CMO of a major soda brand – to their usual steakhouse. Over filet mignons, h...
content:
Let me tell you about the lunch that changed everything for Billboard Bob.

Last Tuesday, Bob took his favorite client – the CMO of a major soda brand – to their usual steakhouse. Over filet mignons, he proudly presented his "tried and true" annual billboard package. 

Then the CMO dropped the bomb: "Bob, we're shifting 40% of our OOH budget to programmatic DOOH and CTV advertising. Our DTC team needs hyper-targeted, performance-based buys."

Enter Digital Dave.

The Clash of Two OOH Eras

Bob's World:

  • 20-year relationships sealed with handshakes
  • Static vinyl placements sold annually
  • Success measured in "eyeballs" and "prestige locations"

Dave's Reality:

  • The soda brand's DTC team uses a self-service OOH advertising platform
  • They buy digital screens by CPM, daypart, and weather conditions
  • Every dollar must drive measurable online conversions

The Wake-Up Call

When Bob checked his pipeline:
✔ 5 traditional clients had reduced budgets
✔ 3 were testing "digital-first" agencies
✔ His best-performing board now had 22% vacancy

How Bob Adapted (Without Abandoning His Strengths)

The Hybrid Pitch

  •  Kept his premium static locations
  •  Added digital extensions: "Your billboard now comes with mobile retargeting"

The Tech Compromise

  • Implemented a simple yield management system
  •  Let Dave handle the programmatic trades

The Surprising Result

  • Traditional clients bought add-ons (30% revenue boost)
  • New DTC brands came through automated platforms
  • Vacancy rates dropped to 4%

What This Means For You

  • Don’t abandon traditional buyers – enhance their buys
  • Make room for digital natives – they’re your growth engine
  • Bridge the gap with:
    • Hybrid packages
    • Simple automation
    • Performance add-ons

"Turns out," Bob admits, "filet mignon tastes better when you're not sweating vacancies."

And just like Bob’s discovered, today's OOH landscape offers three clear paths to revenue - each with its own advantages:

Three Ways Advertisers Want to Buy Now

1. Traditional Placements

  • Still ideal for brand-awareness campaigns
  • Works well with relationship-focused clients
  • Bob's advice: "Keep your iconic locations - but enhance them with digital add-ons"

2. Goal-Based Campaigns

  • Attracts performance-driven advertisers
  • Commands 20-30% premium pricing
  • Dave's tip: "Start with one KPI guarantee (like impressions) before expanding"

3. Non-Guaranteed Deals

  • Fills last-minute inventory automatically
  • Appeals to digital-native buyers
  • Sam's insight: "Programmatic now accounts for 22% of our revenue"

The Tech That Makes It Work

Bob's team implemented:
✔ A unified booking system (keeps traditional and digital deals organized)
✔ Real-time dashboards (shows clients exactly what they’re getting)
✔ Automated reporting (saves 15 hours/week in manual work)

This setup allowed the team to embrace digital out-of-home advertising as a complement to traditional media, not a replacement.

Your 3-Month Adaptation Plan

Month 1: Add digital upsells to existing contracts
Month 2: Train one sales rep on performance metrics
Month 3: Pilot a small programmatic inventory pool

"The biggest surprise?" Bob reflects. "Our traditional clients started asking for digital extensions once they saw the results."

Let’s Grow Together

Change can feel daunting, but remember, you're not alone in this. Many media owners are making this transition successfully, and you can too.

The World Out of Home Organization (WOO) events this year are spotlighting this shift like never before. With key stakeholders, media owners, buyers, and tech enablers coming together in Boston and Mexico, the industry is building a new blueprint for success.

As part of these evolving OOH advertising trends, traditional and digital strategies are blending to meet new buyer demands.

What’s one small step you could take this week to start exploring these new opportunities? Maybe it's researching a new tool or having a conversation with a trusted colleague. Every journey begins with that first step.

Want to chat more about how to make this work for your specific business?

We’ll be at the WOO event, joining fellow media owners and industry leaders to discuss the future of OOH. It’s a great opportunity to exchange ideas and stay ahead of the curve.
author: dinesh

title: Luxury OOH: From Glances to Lasting Impressions
excerpt: By Mandy, Co-founder of MovingWalls In today’s landscape, luxury is no longer just about being noticed, it's about being remembered. When executed strategically, Out-of-Home (OOH) advertising transfo...
content:
By Mandy, Co-founder of MovingWalls

In today’s landscape, luxury is no longer just about being noticed, it's about being remembered. When executed strategically, Out-of-Home (OOH) advertising transforms momentary glances into lasting impressions.

For generations, luxury has embodied more than just premium pricing. It has stood for timeless craftsmanship, deep emotional resonance, and iconic experiences. Yet many luxury brands continue to rely on traditional high-profile placements: towering billboards in Times Square, sleek digital panels in Knightsbridge, or heritage-rich displays in Hong Kong. These undeniably signal prestige  but in an era defined by fragmented attention, is visibility alone still enough?

Today’s consumers are constantly on the move, hyper-connected, and seeking authenticity. They aren’t swayed by loud, impersonal messages. They respond to campaigns that are emotionally engaging and contextually relevant. This calls for a new approach to OOH — one that is not just bold, but intelligent, immersive, and deeply human.

Luxury’s most meaningful moments now unfold in real time on bustling streets, in ways that linger long after the experience.

From Presence to Precision

Global cities like New York, London, Hong Kong, Singapore, and Los Angeles are quietly revolutionizing OOH. The focus is shifting from owning premium space to owning the right moment. Enter Programmatic Digital Out-of-Home (pDOOH): a smarter way to reach audiences through content that adapts to location, time, and context.

Imagine a Louis Vuitton campaign in Tokyo’s Omotesando that shifts based on footfall, or a Cartier ad highlighting its signature timepieces during the evening rush in Hong Kong. In London, Burberry’s visuals align with Fashion Week events, while LA’s beauty brands adapt messaging based on UV levels.

This isn’t technology for the sake of novelty. It’s about relevance. Platforms like MovingWalls empower luxury brands to tell more compelling stories with precise timing, location-based intelligence, and creative flexibility.

Experience Is the New Standard

Luxury today is about more than admiration, it's about connection. Affluent consumers seek emotion and engagement, not just status.

OOH has become a dynamic creative canvas. Picture Burberry’s motion-triggered installations in Hong Kong, Gucci’s AR-enabled bus stops in Singapore, or Dior’s reactive storefronts in SoHo. These aren’t traditional ads, they're surprising, interactive experiences that capture attention and invite participation.

OOH gives luxury the power to go beyond being seen; it invites people to engage.

From AI-driven displays to interactive countdowns for exclusive launches, the fusion of technology and storytelling allows luxury brands to surprise, captivate, and emotionally resonate  all within the public realm.

Measuring What Matters

Historically, luxury marketing leaned on intuition, legacy, and image. But today, data doesn’t dilute luxury, it enhances it.

Measurement isn’t about reducing luxury to metrics. It’s about uncovering influence. From QR scans in Covent Garden to footfall analytics in Beverly Hills and search trends in Hong Kong, modern tools offer luxury brands insight into campaign effectiveness without compromising their exclusivity.

By leveraging these insights, luxury brands can evolve their narratives and strategies to be more impactful and responsive.

Innovation as a Signature Move

Pioneering luxury brands are already stretching the limits of OOH. In LA, Dior’s 3D billboards deliver theatrical brilliance. Hermès explores eco-conscious display formats in the UK. Chanel’s motion-sensitive screens in Hong Kong stop pedestrians in their tracks quite literally.

In cities where culture, business, and innovation intersect, OOH transforms into a canvas for creativity. Luxury, once defined by tradition, is now embracing bold new forms of expression.

The Road Ahead

As the media continues to transform, luxury finds itself at a pivotal moment. Through smarter targeting, immersive storytelling, and measurable outcomes, OOH can evolve into a powerful stage for brand narratives that transcend the streetscape.

The next unforgettable luxury moment may not happen inside a boutique or at a fashion show; it may unfold on a city screen, in a way that’s surprising, emotional, and truly memorable.

About the Author

Mandy Negi is the Co-founder and Managing Director, Adtech at MovingWalls, an advertising technology company that enables top brands to connect with audiences on the move. A strong advocate for digital transformation in media, she blends innovation with impact, driving real-world engagement through cutting-edge technology. Recognized as a Key Opinion Leader, Mandy leads global enterprise software sales and strategic market expansion.
author: dinesh

title: Why Transformation is Key for DOOH to Unlock Big Revenue
excerpt: With the WOO Global Congress in Mexico City fast approaching, one of the OOH industry’s most influential global events is setting the stage for deep conversations on the future of media. As industry l...
content:
With the WOO Global Congress in Mexico City fast approaching, one of the OOH industry’s most influential global events is setting the stage for deep conversations on the future of media. As industry leaders gear up to meet, we want to shine a spotlight on the major transformation happening in out-of-home (OOH) media. For media owners navigating this rapidly evolving landscape, the shift is not on the horizon; it's already here. This change is redefining who buys OOH, how campaigns are executed, and what success truly looks like, reflecting the latest OOH advertising trends.

This article kicks off a special series exploring how AI and digital transformation are propelling digital out-of-home advertising to the forefront of global media innovation. Let’s dive into what’s changing and how you can profitably adapt, whether you’re just starting with DOOH or optimizing your existing screens with digital signage for media owners.

Why OOH is Evolving And Why That’s Good News for Media Owners

OOH used to be all about static billboards and long-term contracts. That model worked well for decades, but the landscape has expanded, and so have the possibilities.

Thanks to digital and automation, media owners now have more tools, and more ways to grow revenue:

✔ Digital screens run multiple campaigns, updated in real-time without any manual effort
✔ Advertisers are more varied, including small businesses and data-driven marketers who value precision
✔ Campaigns can adapt on the spot, triggered by local conditions like weather, traffic, or even sports scores

What’s happening now isn’t just about keeping up with tech. It’s about tapping into a smarter, faster, and more profitable way to run your OOH business, and developing stronger media owner advertising strategies.

Meet the Characters Behind OOH's Transformation

To bring these changes to life, we’re introducing the personalities shaping OOH's future. Think of them as your industry avatars:

  • Billboard Bob - The veteran who built his career on static placements and steakhouse deals
  • Neon Nancy - The creative director who loves iconic signage but struggles with data-driven ads
  • Digital Dave - The tech evangelist who lives for programmatic trading and real-time optimization
  • Street-Smart Sam - The pragmatic media owner who's successfully bridging old and new
  • AI Amy - The forward-thinker automating campaigns with machine learning
  • QR Code Quincy - The innovator connecting physical ads to digital experiences
  • Guerilla Gary - The rule-breaker proving unconventional OOH still has its place

You’ll see them throughout this series, each navigating the digital transformation in their own way.

5 Shifts Defining the Future of OOH Media

As the WOO Global Congress approaches, one thing is clear: transformation in OOH is no longer a prediction—it’s a present reality. Here’s how the landscape is shifting, and what it means for media owners preparing to lead the next wave.

1. When Your Clientele Expanded

Billboard Bob used to wine and dine Fortune 500 brands to land year-long deals. Now, Digital Dave is signing up local juice bars through self-serve platforms - and they’re paying premium CPMs for hyper-targeted dayparts.

Takeaway: Traditional buyers still matter, but digital-native advertisers represent your fastest-growing revenue streams.

2.  When Your Product Got Smarter

Neon Nancy takes pride in her iconic static displays. Meanwhile, AI Amy is running campaigns that change automatically with weather, traffic, or time of day, delivering 32% better engagement.

Takeaway: Classic locations still draw crowds, but dynamic capabilities let you charge premium rates and present opportunities for upgrading static billboards effectively.

3. When Data Became Your Ally

Guerilla Gary measured success by how many posters got torn down. Street-Smart Sam now uses foot traffic sensors to prove 85% dwell time at his prime locations.

Takeaway: Hard metrics help justify price increases and win skeptical digital buyers, a key tactic in programmatic OOH advertising.

4. When Your Backend Needed Upgrading

Billboard Bob's assistant used to track campaigns in a spreadsheet. Digital Dave's automated platform resolves 90% of delivery issues before clients notice.

Takeaway: Automation isn’t about saving time, it’s about building resilient, scalable operations that clients can trust.

5. When Your Team Needed New Skills

Neon Nancy sold based on location ("50K daily views!"). QR Code Quincy now trains teams to sell audience reach and interactive experiences.

Takeaway: Upskilling your sales team delivers the highest ROI of any investment.

Dive Deeper Into Each Shift

These five shifts aren’t just passing trends - they’re clear signals of the future direction of OOH.

With global discussions gaining momentum ahead of the WOO Congress, now is the perfect moment to rethink how you present your inventory, harness your data, and prepare your teams for what’s next in the OOH landscape.

Coming up next: "From Steakhouse Deals to Self-Service: How the OOH Buyer Landscape is Evolving" (featuring Billboard Bob vs. Digital Dave)

📩 Stay tuned—this is just the beginning. More insights to follow as the series unfolds.
author: dinesh

title: An Exclusive Evening of Sake, Strategy, and OOH Innovation in Singapore
excerpt: What an unforgettable evening it was in Singapore on the 6th of March 2025, at our Sake & Media Strategy Event in Singapore, where 30 industry professionals gathered to explore the future of Japan...
content:
What an unforgettable evening it was in Singapore on the 6th of March 2025, at our Sake & Media Strategy Event in Singapore, where 30 industry professionals gathered to explore the future of Japan's OOH landscape and its global impact. 

The event began with an opening address by the esteemed Koichi Inaba-san, Senior Corporate Officer – Deputy Director General of Media Solution HQ, Head of Digital Signage Business Division, and Director of MASTRUM, setting the tone for a remarkable evening. This was followed by expert presentations from Takayuki Owada-san (Deputy Manager – jeki JR East) and Srikanth Ramachandran (Founder & Group CEO – MovingWalls Group), who shared valuable insights into the groundbreaking innovations of MASTRUM and GLOOH (Global Out-of-Home) respectively. Special mention to Masaru Yamaguchi-san, Director – Center of Promotion – MASTRUM, whose contributions were vital for the success of the event.

Takayuki Owada-san introduced MASTRUM, powered by MovingWalls, is an OOH platform operated by East Japan Marketing & Communications (Jeki), a DSP/SSP company. MASTRUM supports not only transit advertising but also street advertising, retail media, as well as analog media such as hanging posters and outdoor sheet advertising. What makes MASTRUM truly unique is its exclusive programmatic inventory across Japan's extensive railway network and diverse venue types including retail locations and transit hubs. 

The platform is seamlessly connected to leading DSPs including DV360, The Trade Desk, Stackadapt, and Mediasmart, alongside MovingWalls’ MAX; a comprehensive universal OOH planning, optimization & reporting platform, and MASTRUM's own DSP, enabling advertisers to easily access and purchase premium Japanese OOH inventory. Leveraging data powered by KDDI - a leading Japanese telecommunications company - along with proprietary datasets, MASTRUM offers sophisticated audience targeting capabilities and robust measurement solutions, making it the premier choice for brands looking to connect with high-traffic audiences throughout Japan.

Srikanth Ramachandran presented GLOOH (Global Out-of-Home), a powerful & strategic initiative, designed to enable seamless, data-driven, and measurable OOH campaigns across multiple global markets. By combining real-time insights, programmatic OOH, and access to extensive digital screen inventories across key global hubs in APAC, MENA, and the Americas including New York, Singapore, London, and Tokyo, GLOOH empowers brands to execute cross-border campaigns with precision and scale. With a proven track record of delivering automated and transparent advertising solutions for global brands, MovingWalls' GLOOH is revolutionizing how advertisers engage with audiences, improving media efficiency and maximizing return on investment.

From China's CNY celebration campaign across Africa to Indonesia's metro lines featuring JBL's latest products from Singapore, GLOOH has powered impactful campaigns in diverse environments. In the past year alone, screens across Africa, Southeast Asia, and the US have showcased content that proves OOH advertising's evolution beyond just location to deliver measurable global impact.

With strategic hubs like Singapore, Hong Kong, and Tokyo leading this transformation, the advertising industry is embracing a borderless approach where data-driven decisions help brands achieve unprecedented visibility and engagement across markets.

What's Next?
As the OOH landscape continues to evolve, now is the time to rethink your advertising strategy. Whether you're looking to leverage MASTRUM's exclusive programmatic inventory in Japan and expand globally with GLOOH's cross-border capabilities, we can help take your brand to the next level. Get in touch with us today!
author: dinesh

title: From Impressions to Impact How MovingWalls Transforms OOH Measurement
excerpt: Did you know the global Out-of-Home (OOH) market has already surpassed $41.82 billion , with Asia contributing a significant $18.11 billion to that total in 2025? This growth is fueled by one undeniab...
content:
Did you know the global Out-of-Home (OOH) market has already surpassed $41.82 billion, with Asia contributing a significant $18.11 billion to that total in 2025? This growth is fueled by one undeniable trend: rapid urbanization. As cities around the world continue to expand at an unprecedented pace, the competition for consumer attention grows even fiercer. Yet, with that challenge comes exciting opportunities for advertisers.

As urban populations rise, so do consumer choices, and we are evolving OOH advertising to meet these changes head-on. It’s no longer just about visibility; it’s about creating interactive, contextually relevant experiences that engage consumers in meaningful ways. Cities have transformed from simple physical spaces into vibrant hubs of advertising potential, offering brands the ability to connect with audiences at every touchpoint and turning the urban environment into a dynamic, immersive canvas for innovation.

As we step into 2025, rapid urbanization and digital technologies are reshaping how brands engage with audiences. The future of OOH is no longer confined to billboards. It’s about creating dynamic, measurable, and interactive experiences. At MovingWalls, we are at the forefront of this shift, driving key disruptions in the OOH industry and unveiling new opportunities that help brands, media owners, and agencies navigate these changes. In the following sections, we’ll explore these disruptions and the new avenues they open for brands to connect with their audiences.

5 Key Areas of Disruption Affecting OOH Advertising

We have identified five major disruptions reshaping brand strategies and audience engagement.

Disruption #1 - Pricing & Accessibility

Historically, OOH advertising was seen as an expensive medium, but DOOH has transformed the landscape. With flexible pricing models and programmatic buying, advertisers of all sizes can now leverage DOOH to optimize their media spend and maximize ROI. Automated buying expands reach, and increased competition has driven costs down, making OOH more accessible than ever before.

Disruption #2 - Precision & Targeting

Advanced technologies such as audience measurement, geo-location data, and real-time bidding allow us to target audiences with unprecedented precision. Through data-driven insights, we can map audience movements, identify key locations, and optimize placements for higher engagement. Our Demand Side Platform (DSP) facilitates audience-based campaigns, ensuring brands connect with the right consumers at the right time.

Disruption #3- Accurate Measurement

Measurement has long been a challenge in OOH, but advanced tools are now closing the gap by providing real-time insights, precise attribution, and deeper audience analytics.

  • Real-time audience insights: Dynamic planning data, independent verification, and impression multipliers provide live visibility into audience reach and demographics.
  • In-campaign optimization: Dynamic content delivery allows advertisers to adjust messaging in real time for maximum impact.
  • Comprehensive post-campaign reports: Performance analytics break down audience demographics, engagement, and overall campaign success.
  • Attribution and conversion tracking: Advanced attribution models link OOH campaigns to conversions, ensuring measurable impact.
  • Deeper audience insights: Mobile data enhances audience profiling, providing granular details on movement patterns and behaviors.
  • Accurate ROI measurement: Data-driven metrics refine return-on-investment calculations, validating campaign effectiveness.
  • Brand lift surveys: Key KPIs like brand awareness and purchase intent are measured, offering quantifiable proof of DOOH campaign success.

Disruption #4 - Engagement & Interactivity

OOH has transformed from a static medium into an interactive platform, enabling real-time consumer engagement. Features like QR codes, social media integrations, and feedback loops allow brands to create dynamic experiences that drive audience participation. These innovations strengthen brand connections and enhance engagement, making OOH more impactful than ever.

Disruption #5- Intent & Integrated Media Planning

Out-of-Home (OOH) advertising now integrates with digital campaigns, creating a unified customer journey. High-impact locations and 3D ads enhance reach, while OOH retargeting, QR codes, and dynamic content boost engagement. Hyperlocal messaging drives footfall by delivering location-specific ads.

Brand affinity strengthens through user stories, influencer content, and testimonials. POI-driven targeting helps brands identify high-performing locations – such as shopping streets and malls – and tailor messaging for better DOOH performance. Integrating DOOH with digital campaigns amplifies messages across platforms, ensuring consistency and measurable results. DOOH data enables mobile retargeting by capturing device IDs, building lookalike audiences, and delivering personalized offers. Data aggregators further refine audience targeting, optimizing campaign performance.

Future Predictions: The Next Phase of OOH

The next phase of OOH advertising will be defined by several key trends:

  • Commerce-Enabled Screens: Every screen will serve as a commerce platform.
  • Programmatic Advertising: Automation will become the standard.
  • Increased Interactivity: More engagement-driven formats will emerge.
  • Measurable Screens: Every screen will be equipped with analytics capabilities.

Sustainability: The industry will prioritize measurable, retail-driven solutions that enhance ROAS while integrating eco-friendly practices.

The Future of OOH is Here with MovingWalls

The OOH industry is transforming, and MovingWalls is at the forefront, enabling brands to leverage data-driven, measurable, and high-impact advertising. OOH is no longer reserved for big brands with massive budgets.

Moving Audience Xchange (MAX) platform makes it accessible to all, redefining audience engagement, improving measurement, and expanding reach across the ecosystem. This shift isn’t just about digital transformation – it’s about seizing the future. Those who embrace these changes will lead the next wave of innovation. MovingWalls equips brands, media owners, and agencies with the tools to navigate and thrive in this evolving landscape.
Ready to take your OOH strategy to the next level? Contact us today and lead the change with MovingWalls.

title: Empowering Singaporean Educational Institutions with Automated Content Management Solutions

content:
As Singapore grows into Asia's premier educational hub, educational institutions, large universities, and bustling schools face the challenge of effectively managing communication with students, parents, faculty, and staff. These diverse groups require timely and relevant information, yet the fast-paced flow of updates and announcements can overwhelm this task. In a world that prioritizes digital engagement, many educational institutions are turning to outdoor advertising powered by digital Content Management Systems (CMS) to streamline communication, strengthen community bonds, and improve operational efficiency.

Why Singaporean Educational Institutions Need Outdoor Advertising Automation?

Singapore’s educational institutions are increasingly relying on CMS-driven outdoor signage to meet their communication demands. Schools and colleges can use digital displays to share everything from safety warnings and event notifications to motivational quotes and academic accomplishments. With automated CMS platforms, institutions save on administrative workloads by instantly updating content, ensuring timely dissemination of important information. Community partnerships within these systems also create an opportunity for additional revenue by allowing ad placements for local businesses.

How Automated Content Management Enhances Communication through Outdoor Advertising

CMS solutions feature tools that allow Singaporean educational institutions to maximize the impact of outdoor signage while ensuring timely and relevant communication. Platforms like LMX consolidate content management and ad inventory into a single interface, empowering schools to control both institutional and community messaging with real-time updates. 

In addition to operational efficiency, LMX supports revenue generation by enabling institutions to blend local business ads with their messages. Its automated scheduling and content approval tools provide flexibility, allowing schools to maintain a meaningful brand presence in the community while benefiting from a steady income source.

Benefits of Automated Content Management in Outdoor Advertising for Educational Institutions

Institutions that use CMS-driven outdoor signage benefit from more than just message broadcasting. CMS solutions enable real-time content management, and scheduling, improving communication efficiency by reducing the need for manual updates and ensuring timely notifications to students, staff, and the wider community.

CMS platforms also strengthen school identity and community engagement by providing a consistent platform to share accomplishments, values, and unique qualities. Through optimized content scheduling, schools can reinforce their brand presence within the community, highlighting their identity and role in meaningful ways.

Additionally, CMS offers new revenue opportunities for schools. By allowing local businesses to advertise on digital displays, schools can fund various initiatives, with the CMS ensuring a balance between essential school content and ad space.

Integrating Outdoor Advertising CMS into Educational Institution Infrastructure

Introducing a CMS-based outdoor signage solution into an educational institution’s infrastructure requires careful planning, from selecting the right hardware to training key staff members. Schools and universities can first assess their needs and identify the audiences they serve to make informed decisions on display types and content strategy. Selecting a CMS with flexible scheduling and built-in analytics ensures that the system remains user-friendly, allowing for a smooth implementation process. With analytics providing insights into which types of content drive the most engagement, institutions can continuously optimize their communication approach, ensuring that displays remain relevant, timely, and aligned with the institution’s objectives.

Overcoming Common Challenges in Outdoor Advertising

While automated outdoor digital signage offers clear advantages, challenges like budget constraints, content overload, and data privacy are crucial to consider. Automated outdoor digital signage, powered by an efficient CMS provides significant cost-saving benefits. Traditionally, managing content required manual labor, such as assigning staff to physically update messages or transport information to specific departments. Effective CMS scheduling helps prevent content overload, keeping screens clear, targeted, and engaging.

Data privacy is also crucial, especially given compliance requirements under Singapore’s Personal Data Protection Act (PDPA). Schools can work with CMS providers like LMX, who prioritize data security to maintain community trust. Additionally, technical support and maintenance are vital to ensure the longevity and uninterrupted functionality of both digital displays and CMS software.

Future-Ready Outdoor Advertising for Singaporean Educational Institutions

With LMX’s integrated approach to content management, Singaporean educational institutions can transform their outdoor signage into powerful communication channels. As schools increasingly depend on digital solutions for community engagement, LMX’s automated CMS offers the functionality and flexibility needed to deliver relevant content effectively and efficiently.

Through LMX, educational institutions enhance communication and build meaningful connections within their community. As Singapore’s educational landscape evolves, tools like LMX ensure that institutions remain at the forefront of impactful, efficient communication, addressing both current and future outdoor advertising needs. title: Future of Digital Out-of-Home (DOOH) Marketing
excerpt: The growth of technology and connectivity has made digital out-of-home (DOOH) advertising a key player in the advertising world, reaching billions of people globally. These digital screens are now com...
content:
The growth of technology and connectivity has made digital out-of-home (DOOH) advertising a key player in the advertising world, reaching billions of people globally. These digital screens are now common in urban areas, capturing the attention of passersby and turning them into potential customers. DOOH marketing goes beyond traditional advertising by transforming static billboards into dynamic displays filled with colorful visuals and engaging content.

The Growth of DOOH Marketing

DOOH is becoming increasingly popular in advertising due to its rapid growth. According to IAB Compass, it is expected that by 2027, DOOH will account for 75% of the out-of-home (OOH) advertising market. Alexei Moltchan from Verve Group notes that Advancements in AI and AR will create new digital ad formats, like holograms, making everything outside your home an advertising opportunity. This shift to DOOH reflects advancements in technology and changing consumer behaviors. Brands are realizing they can engage audiences in new ways, paving the way for innovative advertising strategies.

What is Digital Out-of-Home (DOOH)?

Digital out-of-home (DOOH) is emerging as a popular evolution within the traditional out-of-home (OOH) advertising space. While static billboards remain significant, DOOH enhances the medium with internet-connected digital screens that can display ads or videos on demand. Each screen can be individually controlled, enabling real-time content delivery and creating visually striking billboards and interactive displays. This innovative medium is revolutionizing public spaces, making it a powerful instrument for effective advertising.

The Evolution of Traditional OOH Advertising

Traditional out-of-home (OOH) advertising continues to offer extensive reach through static billboards, posters, and transit ads in high-traffic areas. DOOH, on the other hand, brings in the benefits of digital innovation, enabling real-time updates and enhanced targeting while complementing the strengths of traditional OOH media. For example, interactive bus shelters, and digital displays in malls can tailor ads based on the time of day and the specific audience demographics, enhancing their effectiveness.

Types of DOOH Advertising

Digital out-of-home (DOOH) advertising has transformed how brands connect with consumers in public spaces. Here are the three main types of DOOH advertising:

  • Place-Based Media: Place-based DOOH media is strategically located in specific environments to effectively reach relevant audiences.  Examples include digital billboards in office premises, educational institutions, entertainment, dining, and interactive kiosks.
  • Large Format DOOH: Large format DOOH media utilize extensive digital displays to maximize visibility and engagement in prominent locations. Examples include digital billboards on the highway and digital screens in transit.
  • Point of Purchase (POP): POP media is positioned to influence consumer behavior at the moment of decision-making. Examples include displays at gas stations, supermarkets, convenience stores, shopping malls, and parking areas.

Each of these types of DOOH advertising is designed to engage audiences effectively in different contexts and locations, providing personalized experiences that drive brand impact.

Measurement Techniques for DOOH Advertisements

To evaluate the success of DOOH ads, effective measurement techniques are essential. These methods use advanced technology and data analytics to provide insights into audience engagement and help measure return on investment (ROI). Key measurement techniques include:

  • Impressions and Reach: Employing advanced technology and third-party data, to estimate the number of people passing by or viewing the digital screen. This approach enhances audience measurement by providing insights into viewers demographics and behavioral patterns.
  • Real-Time Measurements: Tracking viewer engagement through metrics like impressions and ad plays are useful for evaluating campaign performance. This approach makes it easier to make quick changes to improve audience engagement and maximize outcomes.
  • Targeting Capabilities: Displays ads based on the environment (e.g., weather conditions, time of day) and uses data analytics to target specific audience segments based on location, demographics, and behavioral insights.
  • Multimedia Support: Dynamic content integration allows ads to be updated and customized dynamically, adapting content based on real-time data or other factors.

Brands can enhance the impact of their DOOH campaigns, optimize their strategies, and reach consumers more effectively by utilizing these measurement approaches.

Why LMX for DOOH Advertising?

LMX offers several benefits that enhance DOOH campaigns.

  • Simplified Buying Process: A connected marketplace that streamlines the planning, buying, and measuring campaigns across various providers.
  • Standardized Measurements and Metrics: Tools for consistent measurement and reporting, including interactive dashboards and a dynamic impression multiplier. These tools provide actionable insights, helping media owners increase revenue.
  • Enhanced Stability: Cloud infrastructure ensures stable and scalable solutions for media owners.
  • Precise targeting and attribution: Using location data and analytics for accurate targeting improves campaign effectiveness. 

These advantages make LMX a preferred choice for media owners looking to maximize their DOOH campaigns.

Unlocking Your DOOH Strategy with LMX

At Location Media Xchange, we provide media owners with advanced tools to navigate the evolving advertising landscape. Our solutions include programmatic transactions, audience analytics, and measurement tools designed to enhance your DOOH campaigns effectively..

With our expertise in data-driven targeting and integration across channels, media owners can create engaging advertisements tailored to their audience. LMX helps simplify processes, boost revenue, and stay competitive in the dynamic world of DOOH advertising. Contact us today to learn how LMX can help your business succeed in Digital Out-of-Home advertising.

title: Unlocking Success: Navigating the Costs of Programmatic Out-of-Home Advertising in APAC
excerpt: Running a programmatic Out-of-Home (OOH) advertising campaign can effectively reach your target audience. However, understanding the costs associated with such campaigns is crucial for direct clients...
content:
Running a programmatic Out-of-Home (OOH) advertising campaign can effectively reach your target audience. However, understanding the costs associated with such campaigns is crucial for direct clients and advertising agencies. This blog post delves into the various factors influencing the cost of programmatic OOH campaigns. 

Understanding Programmatic OOH Advertising

Programmatic OOH advertising leverages technology to automate the buying and selling of advertising space in physical locations. This approach allows advertisers to target specific audiences based on data-driven insights, optimizing campaign performance. However, several elements contribute to the overall cost of running these campaigns.

Key Cost Factors

  1. Inventory Costs: The cost of securing advertising space varies significantly based on the location, size, and visibility of the ad placements. Prime locations in urban centers typically command higher prices due to increased foot traffic.
  2. Technology Fees: Utilizing a programmatic platform often incurs technology fees. These costs can include subscription fees for access to software that enables real-time bidding and audience targeting.
  3. Data Costs: Effective targeting relies on high-quality data. Accessing third-party data sources for audience insights can add to the overall expenses.
  4. Creative Production: Developing engaging and effective creative content is essential for any campaign. Depending on the complexity, size, and quality required, production costs can vary widely.
  5. Measurement and Analytics: To gauge campaign effectiveness, investing in measurement tools is necessary. These tools help analyze performance metrics and audience engagement, which can influence future campaigns.

Budgeting for Success

When budgeting for a programmatic OOH campaign, it’s essential to consider both fixed and variable costs. Fixed costs include technology subscriptions and creative production, while variable costs might fluctuate based on inventory selection and data usage.

A well-structured budget should also account for potential fluctuations in inventory prices due to demand spikes or seasonality. This flexibility allows advertisers to adjust their strategies in response to changing market conditions.

Benefits of Programmatic OOH

Despite the associated costs, programmatic OOH advertising offers several advantages:

  • Precision Targeting: Advertisers can reach specific demographics based on real-time data insights.
  • Flexibility: Campaigns can be adjusted to respond to market changes quickly.
  • Enhanced Measurement: Advanced analytics provide deeper insights into campaign performance, enabling continuous optimization.

The Role of MovingWalls

As a leader in the AdTech industry, MovingWalls provides a comprehensive platform that simplifies the complexities of programmatic OOH advertising. Their innovative technology automates planning, buying, executing, and measuring campaigns, making it easier for brands and agencies to navigate the landscape.

MovingWalls offers unique features such as:

  • Real-Time Monitoring: Advertisers can track campaign performance in real-time, allowing for immediate adjustments.
  • Data-Driven Insights: The platform utilizes advanced analytics to provide actionable insights into audience behavior.
  • Seamless Integration: MovingWalls connects advertisers with extensive inventory across multiple markets, ensuring optimal reach.

Furthermore, MovingWalls is committed to enhancing transparency within the AdTech ecosystem. Advertisers can make informed decisions that maximize their ROI by providing clear reporting metrics and performance indicators. This level of insight is particularly valuable in the dynamic APAC region, where consumer behavior can shift rapidly.

With a focus on empowering advertisers through technology, MovingWalls stands out as an essential partner for any organization looking to maximize their OOH advertising effectiveness.

Investing in a programmatic OOH campaign requires careful consideration of various cost factors. However, the potential benefits—such as enhanced targeting capabilities and improved measurement—can lead to significant returns on investment.

title: Content Management for Digital Signage in Indian Healthcare: Trends, Challenges, and Solutions
excerpt: Digital signage is revolutionizing the healthcare industry in India, enhancing patient engagement, operational efficiency, and overall communication within healthcare facilities. With the rise of digi...
content:
Digital signage is revolutionizing the healthcare industry in India, enhancing patient engagement, operational efficiency, and overall communication within healthcare facilities. With the rise of digital transformation in healthcare, the deployment of digital signage is no longer a luxury but a necessity. However, managing the content displayed on these signs can be challenging, especially in a sector where information security and accuracy are paramount. 

Evolving Trends in Digital Signage Across Indian Healthcare

The use of digital signage in Indian healthcare is growing rapidly, driven by the need for better patient communication and experience. Healthcare facilities are increasingly adopting digital signage for various purposes, including patient education and internal communication. This trend aligns with the global movement towards digital health, where technology plays a crucial role in improving healthcare delivery.

A significant trend is the integration of digital signage with other healthcare systems, such as Patient Information Display Systems (PIDS) and billing systems. This integration enables real-time updates on patient status, doctor availability, and other critical information, enhancing the overall patient experience. Additionally, digital signage is increasingly being used to display infotainment content, providing patients and visitors with engaging and informative material during their wait times.

Unlocking the Potential of Digital Signage in Healthcare

Digital signage plays a crucial role in enhancing the quality of care and overall patient satisfaction. By providing timely and relevant information, digital signage helps keep patients informed and engaged, whether it’s through educational content, health tips, or updates on hospital services. Moreover, the presence of digital signage in large healthcare facilities aids in wayfinding, helping patients and visitors navigate complex premises with ease, thereby reducing stress and confusion.

Another significant advantage is its role in effective queue management. By integrating digital signage with queue management systems, healthcare facilities can provide real-time updates on wait times, helping to reduce patient frustration and improve satisfaction. Additionally, digital signage serves as a powerful tool for internal communication, ensuring that staff members are promptly informed about important updates, protocols, and announcements. 

The automation of content updates and the seamless integration of digital signage with existing healthcare systems not only streamline operations but also reduce the workload on staff, allowing them to focus on delivering better patient care.

In essence, digital signage is not just a communication tool; it’s a strategic asset that enhances the overall efficiency of healthcare operations and significantly improves patient experience.

Overcoming the Challenges of Managing Digital Signage Content

While the benefits of digital signage in healthcare are clear, managing the content on these displays comes with its own set of challenges, particularly in a sector where information security and accuracy are critical.

Healthcare facilities must ensure that the content displayed is secure and compliant with regulations. Insecure content management systems (CMS) can lead to data breaches or unauthorized access, putting sensitive patient information at risk. Additionally, the relevance and timeliness of the content are paramount. Outdated or inaccurate information can lead to confusion, dissatisfaction, and operational inefficiencies.

Moreover, the integration of digital signage with other healthcare systems requires a robust and flexible CMS capable of handling real-time data and ensuring accurate display across multiple screens and locations. Lastly, given the busy nature of healthcare environments, the CMS must be user-friendly and efficient, enabling staff to manage and update content with minimal effort and time.

The challenges may be significant, but they are not insurmountable. Healthcare facilities that effectively address these issues can unlock the full potential of digital signage and significantly 

Why LMX Content is the Ideal Solution for Healthcare Digital Signage

Managing digital signage content in healthcare settings requires a solution that is both powerful and intuitive. Location Media Xchange's CMS, LMX Content, is designed to meet the specific needs of the healthcare industry, addressing the challenges head-on and enabling healthcare providers to leverage the full potential of digital signage.

LMX Content ensures secure and compliant content distribution across all digital signage, secure transmission and access controls. This security feature guarantees that only authorized personnel can manage and update the content, thus minimizing the risk of data breaches or unauthorized access.

The CMS also provides a reliable and user-friendly interface that simplifies content management. With features like playlists, scheduling, and permission-based user roles, LMX Content ensures that content remains relevant, timely, and up-to-date, all while reducing the administrative burden on the communications team.

By automating many aspects of content management, LMX Content allows the healthcare industry to focus on what matters most—patient care. The platform’s intuitive design tools also make it easy to create engaging content that adheres to healthcare branding guidelines, while its robust scheduling features ensure that updates are deployed efficiently and effectively.

Here’s an example of how Manipal Hospitals used LMX Content to enhance patient experience: They deployed 20 interactive screens in high-traffic areas, offering localized health tips, service information, and personalized greetings. This led to a 35% increase in patient engagement, reduced staff workload by addressing common queries, and improved the hospital’s modern image while opening up revenue opportunities through advertising.

Driving the Future of Patient Engagement and Operational Efficiency

As the Indian healthcare industry continues to evolve, digital signage will play an increasingly important role in enhancing patient engagement and operational efficiency. However, managing the content displayed on these signs requires a solution that is secure, reliable, and easy to use.
